# Vesting Explained: Schedules, Cliffs, and Acceleration (Carta Official)

## Summary
The market standard for startup equity vesting is a four-year vesting period with a one-year cliff: 25% vests at the 12-month anniversary, then the remaining 75% vests in equal monthly installments over the following 36 months. If an employee leaves before the cliff, they forfeit all unvested equity. Double trigger acceleration requires two simultaneous conditions to trigger accelerated vesting (typically: acquisition closing AND termination without cause); this is more protective for founders than single trigger (which accelerates on acquisition alone). Double trigger is the market norm for employee equity; single trigger is more common for advisor equity grants where the advisor is not expected to continue post-acquisition.

## Key quotations / statistics
- "The market standard for startup equity vesting is a four-year vesting period with a one-year cliff"
- "Year 1 (Cliff): 25% of shares vest on the one-year anniversary. If an employee leaves before this cliff, they lose all equity"
- "Years 2-4: The remaining 75% vests in equal monthly or quarterly installments over the next three years"
- Example: "An employee with 4,800 shares would receive 1,200 shares after one year, then 100 shares monthly for the following 36 months"
- Double trigger acceleration: "Two conditions occur simultaneously" to accelerate vesting
- Double trigger "is more commonly offered to advisors who are not expected to continue with the company through major changes like acquisitions"
- One 2026-dated result confirms this vesting model remains current practice (Orrick article dated April 7, 2026)

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- This is the primary source for `guides/06-vesting-schedules.md`. The 4-year/1-year cliff is so universal it qualifies as a convention rather than a recommendation.
- Double trigger vs single trigger: the Carta source's framing (double trigger for advisors) appears to be an edge case of the broader pattern. Cross-reference with the StartupLaw Blog source on double trigger for fuller context.
- For `guides/06-vesting-schedules.md`: include the "100 shares/month" worked example from Carta's content to make the schedule concrete.
- Founders' own vesting (reverse vesting on restricted stock) is a separate but related topic - the peer stinger (incorporation-startup-stack-stinger) covers the 83(b) election on founder restricted stock, which is the founder vesting mechanism.
- Monthly vs quarterly post-cliff vesting should be noted as a negotiation point; monthly is more common and more favorable to employees.
